title: LLM Grounding — Citations, Eval, and the Hallucination Tax
description: From naive RAG to a production-grade grounding stack. Includes hands-on labs in pgvector and a final live design review.

videos.title: Why most RAG demos fail in production
videos.verifyPrompt: Name three failure modes the lecture covered, and which one is hardest to detect from offline evals alone. One paragraph.

videos.title: Designing citation gates that actually catch hallucinations
videos.verifyPrompt: Describe a citation gate you would build for a customer-support agent over a 50k-doc knowledge base. What do you log, and what gets a human in the loop?

title: Design Systems — From Figma File to Production Tokens
description: Build a tokenized, typed, performance-aware design system. Covers a11y, theming, and the politics of getting it adopted.

videos.title: Tokens, not variables: the architectural shift
videos.verifyPrompt: Explain the difference between a primitive token and a semantic token. Give an example of when collapsing them would hurt you.

videos.title: Component API design that survives 18 months
videos.verifyPrompt: Sketch a Button component API. Justify every prop and one prop you deliberately rejected.

title: Kubernetes in Production — Beyond the Helm Chart
description: Operate K8s at scale: chaos, cost, multi-region, secrets. Real production incident postmortems, not Hello World.

videos.title: Why your dev cluster lies to you
videos.verifyPrompt: Describe two prod-only failure modes that dev clusters cannot reproduce and how you would simulate them in CI.

videos.title: Reading the incident: a real postmortem walkthrough
videos.verifyPrompt: Outline the timeline of a multi-AZ outage and which observability signal would have shortened MTTR by the most.

title: Product Strategy for AI-First Products
description: How to scope, validate, and ship AI features that don't get sunset in 6 months. Live critiques of real teardowns.

videos.title: The wrapper trap and how to avoid it
videos.verifyPrompt: Name a popular AI-feature category that is mostly a wrapper. Describe how a team could escape the trap within one quarter.
